nodes.label: Second Brain Integration Pattern
nodes.author: Dung Nguyen
nodes.rationale: KB stores synthesized AI-readable context; second-brain holds raw daily notes. Split prevents AI context overload while preserving detail.

nodes.label: AI-SDLC 3-Layer Evidence Model (L1 Dev + L2 QC + L3 QA)
nodes.author: Dung Nguyen
nodes.rationale: 3-layer evidence model enforces accountability at each handoff: Dev self-certifies (L1), QC validates (L2), QA audits smoke test on STG (L3). Each layer is prerequisite for the next.

nodes.label: 3-Layer AI Enforcement Model (Automated Gates + MR Checklist + Sampling Audit)
nodes.rationale: No self-report; layer 1 blocks at push/merge, layer 2 is lightweight MR checklist, layer 3 is random sampling to prevent gaming. Designed to measure actual AI adoption without bias.
nodes.norm_label: 3-layer ai enforcement model (automated gates + mr checklist + sampling audit)

nodes.label: Evidence-Based Auditing — QA reads Redmine without asking Dev
nodes.rationale: QA reads only from Redmine. Missing any item = automatic FAIL. Forces evidence to be self-explanatory and eliminates verbal trust.
nodes.norm_label: evidence-based auditing — qa reads redmine without asking dev

nodes.label: Independent Release Gate — QA autonomy to block production deploy
nodes.rationale: Only CTO/CPO can override QA NO-GO with documented exception. Prevents PM or Dev Lead pressure from bypassing quality standards.
nodes.norm_label: independent release gate — qa autonomy to block production deploy

nodes.label: QA vs QC Role Distinction
nodes.rationale: QC tests directly (bug report, sweep); QA audits evidence and owns Go/No-Go gate. Separation prevents conflict of interest when teams self-assess release readiness.
